summary: Parallel review: FAIL (2 critical, 7 warning, 11 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 `bin/lib/cron.mjs:101` — `staleItem.status = "ready"` mutates the object in-place from the `items` array. The downstream `readyItems` filter at line 106 depends on this mutation. If `listProjectItems` is ever refactored to return frozen/cloned objects, stale items would be silently excluded from the ready pool with no error. Track recovered IDs separately (carry-forward from Simplicity run_3).

🟡 `bin/lib/github.mjs:266` — `setProjectItemStatus` re-calls `gh project item-list` internally even though `cron.mjs` already obtained item IDs from `listProjectItems`. One tick with stale recovery + dispatch makes 4+ redundant `item-list` API calls. The `id` field is available in the `listProjectItems` result (`github.mjs:246`) but not threaded through. Backlog for rate-limit headroom.

🟡 `.team/features/cron-based-outer-loop/tasks/*/artifacts/` — No `test-output.txt` stored in any task; gate output in the spec is truncated and does not include cron-setup results; claimed suite count (555) is not independently verifiable from artifacts — store test runner output as a task artifact (carry-forward from task-4)

🟡 `test/cron-tick.test.mjs` — No CLI subprocess test for `agt cron-setup`; three subprocess tests exist for cron-tick but zero for cron-setup; a regression in the agt.mjs dispatcher would pass all current tests undetected
🟡 `test/cron-tick.test.mjs:399` — Stale+ready coexistence test only asserts `runCalled === true`; dispatch ordering (recovered item dispatched first) is never asserted — carry-forward from task-4
🟡 `bin/lib/cron.mjs:20` — `readProjectNumber` duplicates `outer-loop.mjs:117` body-for-body; extract to a shared utility (carry-forward from prior reviews, not introduced by task-5)
The only carry-forward 🟡 is `readProjectNumber` being duplicated between `cron.mjs:20` and `outer-loop.mjs:117` — it predates task-5 and has been in the backlog since the architect eval on task-4.
